channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Data Management for AI Success: Unlocking Full Potential
item.description: Unlocking full potential of artificial intelligence through modern data management and partnership with industry leaders.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How ATO as code can reduce this compliance burden
item.description: OPM’s Dan Jacobs and stackArmor’s Gaurav “GP” Pal led an ACT-IAC team to develop a maturity model to help implement authority to operate as code. The post How ATO as code can reduce this compliance burden first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: How does Elon Musk define ‘efficiency’? We asked his former employees
item.description: Elon Musk has big plans for his new Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) once Donald Trump takes the White House in January. Prior to the election, the Tesla CEO said he would help cut $2 trillion from the federal budget.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Netflix says 60 million watched Tyson, Paul fight
item.description: About 60 million households tuned in to watch a prime-time prize fight between Jake Paul and Mike Tyson on Friday, Netflix said over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: En el camino hacia la eficiencia digital los bancos apuestan por la IA
item.description: Ante la reducción de las partidas presupuestarias y el aumento de las expectativas de los clientes, los bancos recurren cada vez más a la inteligencia artificial (IA), según un estudio reciente de la consultora Publicis Sapiens.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: One in Five Americans Get News from Digital Influencers on X
item.description: A report by the Pew Research Center finds that one in five Americans regularly get their news from digital influencers on social media platform X, with racial minorities, young adults, and those with lower incomes more likely to rely on these sources.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: AI platforms driving business transformation in the UAE: insights from industry experts
item.description: In today’s fast-paced digital landscape, AI platforms are playing a pivotal role in reshaping industries and driving business transformation.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Kyndryl, Microsoft expand mainframe modernization services
item.description: IT infrastructure services provider Kyndryl continues to build on its partnership with Microsoft, most recently adding new mainframe modernization support, security services, and cloud migration offerings.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Democrats are missing the point on Joe Rogan
item.description: The weeks after an election are open season for pet political theories. An electorate as massive and sprawling as America’s will never swing on just one thing. But that’s no fun to admit. So why not share your one thing—you can be as right as anyone else.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Bien-Kahn, Fast Company

item.title: The Onion CEO explains Infowars purchase amid Alex Jones's claims about auction
item.description: The Onion CEO Ben Collins confirmed that the publication purchased Alex Jones’s Infowars outlet in a Saturday series of posts on the social media platform Bluesky amid Jones's claims of wrongdoing and illegal activity.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Amazon developing driver eyeglasses to shave seconds off deliveries
item.description: Such directions could shave valuable seconds off each delivery by providing left or right directions off elevators and around obstacles such as gates or aggressive dogs.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Australia Considers Raising Minimum Age for Social Media Use
item.description: Australian government proposes to raise minimum age for social media use from 13 to 16, citing need to protect children from online harms.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: For November, Patch Tuesday includes three Windows zero-day fixes
item.description: Microsoft’s November Patch Tuesday release addresses 89 vulnerabilities in Windows, SQL Server, .NET and Microsoft Office — and three zero-day vulnerabilities (CVE-2024-43451, CVE-2024-49019 and CVE-2024-49039) that mean a patch now recommendation for Windows platforms.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Tech group presses Trump to review AI regulations
item.description: A tech lobbying group called on President-elect Trump and his team to ensure the U.S. maintains leadership in the development and deployment of artificial intelligence (AI) as it crafts the incoming regulations and policy on the advancing technology.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Google’s Gemini app is now available on iPhones
item.description: Google has entered a new and more intense phase of the AI wars, introducing its own Google Gemini app for iPhones; now you can use Apple Intelligence, ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot and Google Gemini on one device.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Biden administration finalizes $6.6B in chips grants for TSMC before Trump takes office
item.description: The White House on Friday announced it had finalized an agreement with a major semiconductor manufacturer to invest billions of dollars in facilities in Arizona as President Biden looks to cement parts of his legislative legacy before leaving office in January.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: In the wake of Java Development Kit (JDK) 23, which arrived September 17, work now focuses on the planned successor release, JDK 24, which has ballooned to 21 proposed features.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 18 states sue SEC, Gensler for 'regulatory overreach' on crypto
item.description: Eighteen Republican attorneys general sued the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and Chair Gary Gensler on Thursday for allegedly overstepping the agency’s authority in its enforcement actions against the cryptocurrency industry.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Man who stole and laundered roughly $1B in bitcoin is sentenced to 5 years in prison
item.description: A computer expert who stole bitcoin worth billions of dollars at current prices — and then spent years laundering some of the hacked cryptocurrency with help from his wife — has been sentenced to five years in prison.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kunzelman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: AI is everywhere. How should California schools handle it? Teachers’ different approaches show its potential — and limits
item.description: 'We’re trying to teach the ethical use of these new tools that are just going to keep on growing,' one teacher says.
item.dc:creator: Kristen Taketa, The Mercury News

item.title: Apple’s iPhone partners make plans for US manufacturing
item.description: In a sign of the times, Apple’s key manufacturing partners are ready to ramp up production in the US should the incoming Trump administration keep its promise to levy painful surcharges on Chinese imports.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Understanding Hyperlight, Microsoft’s minimal VM manager
item.description: Virtualization seemed to be one of those technologies that was done. With Hyper-V, VMware, and KVM all offering similar features and performance, all that was left to do was to support new chipset instructions and fix bugs.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: F5 gateway works to protect and manage AI applications
item.description: A new gateway from F5 is aimed at helping enterprises to better manage AI security and delivery. The F5 AI Gateway offers an automated way for customers to secure and manage interactions among AI applications, APIs, and large language models (LLMs).
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: At CBP, automated software testing is integral to the mission
item.description: Some parts of the system are more difficult to automate, including operations like biometric fingerprinting. The post At CBP, automated software testing is integral to the mission first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: Elon Musk responds after 'SNL' star claims he made her cry
item.description: In a now-deleted TikTok post, "Saturday Night Live" star Chloe Fineman said that Elon Musk made her cry when he hosted the show.
item.dc:creator: Darren Sweeney, NewsNation

item.title: Facebook and Instagram users in Europe can opt for less personalized ads
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Facebook and Instagram users in Europe will get the option to see less personalized ads if they don't want to pay for an ad-free subscription, social media company Meta said Tuesday, bowing to pressure from Brussels over privacy and digital competition concerns.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Japan's SoftBank returns to profit after gains at Vision Fund and other investments
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese technology group SoftBank swung back to profitability in the July-September quarter, boosted by positive results in its Vision Fund investments. Tokyo-based SoftBank Group Corp.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, KREX 5
